key_insight: The paper turns numeric tabular datasets into interpretable statistical embeddings so similar datasets can be retrieved and aligned even when their variables do not share names or conventions.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster dataset discovery, model initialization, and algorithm selection for materials and scientific workflows, including sensitive-data settings where raw observations cannot be shared.
method: Compute exploratory data analysis descriptors for each numeric dataset.
Embed descriptors into a shared vector space with a pretrained sentence transformer.
Use CCA, including a penalized sparse variant, to measure similarity and recover interpretable variable-level correspondences; optionally apply differential privacy before embedding.
evidence_signal: Across 15 datasets spanning benchmarks, materials informatics, and nuclear graphite, the approach achieved a total P@1 of 0.9 and remained robust under embedding ablations and differential-privacy budgets.
watch_item: Interpretability depends on the quality and completeness of the chosen descriptors, and privacy or sparse-CCA settings may trade off alignment fidelity against robustness.
paper_title: Statistical Embeddings for Similarity, Retrieval, and Interpretable Alignment of Numeric Tabular Datasets
